Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

I don't really want to make you resubmit all of the patches in this series, but despite my asking several times to preserve the original patch descriptions in these patches as they are updated, this is not being done.

I'm going to mark all of the patches in this series -1, but you could submit trivial follow-on patches to fix up the commit messages of the patches and avoid the need to resubmit all of the patches in this series.

Patch Set 5:

Is it really needed to have an ldiskfs patch series for every vanilla kernel?  I understand that Ubuntu 24.04 will be kernel 6.8, so that one makes sense, but not sure of the value for 6.7?

Patch Set 5:

> Patch Set 5:
> 
> Is it really needed to have an ldiskfs patch series for every vanilla kernel?  I understand that Ubuntu 24.04 will be kernel 6.8, so that one makes sense, but not sure of the value for 6.7?

No need to land this, just keeping the series up-to-date with upstream kernel.

I only think the LTS kernels are worth landing (6.1 / 6.6)

So far 6.7 and 6.8 share the same ldiskfs series, not sure what extra patches will be needed for Ubuntu 24.04.

Patch Set 5: Code-Review-1

Mark -1 for now.  If this isn't landing, then it isn't possible to land patches based on it either.  Either a new 6.8 ldiskfs patch should be created, or this one can be updated to that kernel.

Patch Set 6:

is it possible to have server support for ubuntu24.04 (e.g. 6.8.0-36-generic) instead of upstream 6.7 and 6.8? that would be much better option to run Lustre server on standard linux distribution.

Patch Set 6:

> Patch Set 6:
> 
> is it possible to have server support for ubuntu24.04 (e.g. 6.8.0-36-generic) instead of upstream 6.7 and 6.8? that would be much better option to run Lustre server on standard linux distribution.

Yes, these are 'place holders' to ease porting to the next LTS and starting points for distro kernels.
(Hence the build-only and WIP tags)

commit.subject: LU-16350 ldiskfs: Server support for linux v6.7 / Ubuntu 24.04
commit.message:
LU-16350 ldiskfs: Server support for linux v6.7 / Ubuntu 24.04

Exclude kunit tests [files matching *-test.c] from ldiskfs build

Updated patch series for Linux v6.7:
  ext4-corrupted-inode-block-bitmaps-handling-patches.patch
  ext4-ialloc-uid-gid-and-pass-owner-down.patch

Updated patch series for Linux v6.5:
   ext4-data-in-dirent.patch

Change struct osd_it_ea_dirent.oied_name from zero length
to flexible array so strncmp works as expected.

Test-Parameters: trivial
HPE-bug-id: LUS-11376
Signed-off-by: Shaun Tancheff <shaun.tancheff@hpe.com>
Change-Id: I2b2325a5874a91096fbd63750096e459065668bc
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/54216
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <jsimmons@infradead.org>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Jian Yu <yujian@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Shuichi Ihara <sihara@ddn.com>
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
